data.title: Pokemon cards now digital on-chain, crypto stable amid ETF inflows. Treasury BV raises $147m for BTC, Bitmine buys $358m ETH. ETH supply low on exchanges. Various news on adoption, investment.
data.description: The cryptocurrency market remains stable despite significant inflows into Bitcoin (BTC) Exchange-Traded Funds (ETF). The Federal Reserve is planning a conference focused on stablecoins and tokenisation. American Bitcoin (BTC) experiences a surge following its listing on Nasdaq. Renowned investor Dalio highlights that both BTC and gold are the most effective hedge against a debt crisis. The investment firm Treasury BV secures $147 million for the purchase of BTC. Bitmine invests $358 million in Ethereum (ETH). The supply of ETH on exchanges drops to a three-year low. AlphaTON launches a $100 million TON treasury. SUI Group acquires 20 million SUI tokens. India and the United States lead in global crypto adoption. California's $500 billion pension fund faces division over BTC investments. US Bancorp resumes offering cryptocurrency custody services. Gemini aims for a $2.2 billion valuation in its upcoming initial public offering (IPO). The European Central Bank (ECB) urges stricter regulations for non-EU stablecoins. The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) contemplates a proposal on the quantum-safety of cryptocurrencies. Coinbase sets a target for 50% of its code to be written by artificial intelligence within the year.
IDF spokesman: "We are going to defeat Hamas in Gaza City. What about the day after? Decisions need to be made. If there is no other solution, there will be a military government."
